# SRC069 - Beyond Martech: Building Trust with Consumers

## Metadata

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Section | Ad Trust |
| Authors / source | Nielsen |
| Year | 2021 |
| Source type | Industry trust survey report |
| Quality | Medium-High |
| URL | [https://www.nielsen.com/insights/2021/beyond-martech-building-trust-with-consumers-and-engaging-where-sentiment-is-high/](https://www.nielsen.com/insights/2021/beyond-martech-building-trust-with-consumers-and-engaging-where-sentiment-is-high/) |

## Bias / limitation

Industry survey; useful directional trust data.

## Core takeaway

Recommendations from known people are the most trusted ad/influence channel.

## How the marketing agent should use it

Use to emphasize peer proof, reviews, and word-of-mouth loops.

## Tactics unlocked

- Convert source insight into a campaign hypothesis.
- Translate the hypothesis into one ad angle, one landing-page proof section, and one measurement plan.
- Record whether the tactic worked in `memory/tactic_performance.md`.

## Agent instruction

When this source is relevant, write one sentence explaining **why this source applies to the buyer state** before using it.

## Research extraction template

```markdown
### Source extraction

- Source ID: SRC069
- What it changes about our strategy:
- What it proves:
- What it does not prove:
- Ad tactic suggested:
- Landing page proof suggested:
- Measurement implication:
- Follow-up source needed:
```
